  " Opened via registered T-code
ELSIF sy-tcode = 'SE38' OR sy-tcode = 'SA38'.
  " Opened via editor or direct execution
ENDIF.

" Authorization at T-code level checked before screen opens:
" Role containing S_TCODE with TCD = 'ZMY_SALES'

followupAnswerBank.question: What is SM36 and how is it used with selection screen variants?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SM36 background job definition transaction hai. Yahan tum: Job naam do. ABAP program step add karo. Variant assign karo — variant mein selection screen values stored hain. Job start time set karo — immediately, specific time, ya event-based. Save aur release karo. SM36 mein variant mandatory hota hai background job step mein — kyunki job run hone pe koi user nahi hota selection screen fill karne ke liye, variant se values load hoti hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SM36 is the SAP transaction for defining and scheduling background jobs. When connecting a selection screen report to a background job, the critical step is assigning a variant in the job step definition. The variant stores all the selection screen parameter values that would normally be entered interactively. SM36 allows specifying the ABAP program name, the variant to use, and the start condition — immediate, scheduled time, or event-triggered. After releasing the job, it can be monitored in SM37. Without a variant, the background job cannot supply input to the mandatory selection screen fields and will fail.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Month-end inventory report daily at 11 PM run karna tha. SM36 mein: Job name ZINV_DAILY, Step: ZINVENTORY_REPORT, Variant PROD_DAILY, Start: Daily at 23:00. Released. SM37 mein agle din check kiya — Finished. Output SM37 spool mein milti hai.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: SM36 mein variant assign karna bhool jaana — job fail karta hai 'No variant specified' ya mandatory fields missing error ke saath. Variant field mandatory step definition mein hota hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SM36 Job Setup (conceptual - done via transaction UI):
" Job Name:    ZINV_DAILY
" Step 1:
"   Program:   ZINVENTORY_REPORT
"   Variant:   PROD_DAILY
" Start Time:  Daily at 23:00
" Release:     Yes

" Programmatic job scheduling (alternative to SM36 UI):
DATA: lv_jobname  TYPE tbtcjob-jobname VALUE 'ZINV_DAILY',
      lv_jobcount TYPE tbtcjob-jobcount.

CALL FUNCTION 'JOB_OPEN'
  EXPORTING jobname  = lv_jobname
  IMPORTING jobcount = lv_jobcount.

CALL FUNCTION 'JOB_SUBMIT'
  EXPORTING report   = 'ZINVENTORY_REPORT'
            variant  = 'PROD_DAILY'
            jobname  = lv_jobname
            jobcount = lv_jobcount.

followupAnswerBank.question: What information does SM37 provide about a selection screen report's background execution?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SM37 mein background job ke baare mein milta hai: Job status — Scheduled, Released, Active (running), Finished, Aborted. Actual start time aur end time. Job log — detailed messages jo program ne execute karte waqt generate kiye. Spool list — report ka output jo background mein print hua. Agar job Aborted hai toh job log mein exact error message milta hai — missing variant field, authorization error, dump message. Developer SM37 mein aake diagnose karta hai ki background execution failed kyun.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SM37 displays the execution status of background jobs: Scheduled (waiting), Released (ready to run), Active (currently running), Finished (completed successfully), or Aborted (failed). For each job, SM37 provides the actual start and end times, the job log containing all messages written during execution including ABAP WRITE statements and MESSAGE calls, and the spool output generated by the report. When a job aborts, the job log shows the specific error — such as a missing mandatory variant field, authorization failure, or runtime dump — enabling targeted debugging.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Background job Aborted tha. SM37 mein job log khola: 'Company code field is mandatory (OBLIGATORY)'. Root cause: Variant PROD_DAILY mein p_bukrs field empty tha. Variant edit kiya — p_bukrs = '1000' daala. Job rerun kiya — Finished. 10 minute fix.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: SM37 mein job status 'Finished' dekh ke assume karna ki output sahi hai — Finished sirf yeh batata hai ki job bina ABAP dump ke chali. Spool output bhi check karo ki report ka actual data sahi aaya ya nahi.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SM37 Navigation:
" SM37 -> Enter job name filter -> Execute
" Job list shows: Status | Start Time | End Time | User
" Select job -> Job Log button -> Detailed messages
" Select job -> Spool button -> Report output

" Developer-added log messages visible in SM37 job log:
REPORT zlogged_report.
PARAMETERS: p_bukrs TYPE bukrs OBLIGATORY.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  IF sy-batch = 'X'.
    WRITE: / 'Job started:', sy-datum, sy-uzeit.  " Visible in SM37 log
  ENDIF.
  SELECT * FROM vbak WHERE bukrs = @p_bukrs INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_ord).
  WRITE: / 'Records processed:', lines( lt_ord ).  " In SM37 spool

followupAnswerBank.question: How is SHDB (Screen Recorder) used with selection screens?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SHDB ek screen recorder hai — SAP mein koi bhi transaction perform karo, SHDB sab screen actions record karta hai. Selection screen ke context mein: SHDB se selection screen fields fill karne ka recording karo. Yeh recording BDC (Batch Data Communication) programs ke liye use hoti hai jo automated selection screen interaction karte hain. Testing automation ke liye bhi SHDB recordings use hoti hain — ECATT ke saath. Specific use case: Agar bahut reports ko same selection screen fields ke saath run karna hai — SHDB recording se BDC program banao jo automatically fields fill kare aur execute kare.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SHDB is the SAP screen recorder transaction that records all screen interactions including selection screen field entries and button clicks. In the context of selection screens, SHDB is used to record the exact screen field names and their values when filling a selection screen — this recording forms the basis of BDC programs that automate report execution or for ECATT test scripts that automate selection screen testing. SHDB captures the dynpro number, screen field names, and values, which developers use to write automated BDC or ECATT scripts.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Client ne requirement diya: Har month-end pe 20 different plant codes ke liye same report manually run karna. SHDB se selection screen interaction record kiya. ABAP BDC program banaya jo loop mein sab plant codes ke saath selection screen fill kare aur report submit kare. Manual 20 executions → 1 automated BDC run.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: SHDB recording mein field technical naam se dhundna — SHDB automatically correct field names capture karta hai. Lekin agar screen change hoti hai (new fields added, old removed) toh recording obsolete ho jaati hai — re-record karna padta hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SHDB Recording usage in BDC for selection screen:
" SHDB records: BDC_OKCODE, BDC_CURSOR, field names, values

DATA: lt_bdcdata TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bdcdata.

" Fill BDC table from SHDB recording:
APPEND VALUE #( program  = 'SAPLSSEL'
                dynpro   = '1000'
                dynbegin = 'X' ) TO lt_bdcdata.
APPEND VALUE #( fnam     = 'P_BUKRS'
                fval     = '1000' ) TO lt_bdcdata.
APPEND VALUE #( fnam     = 'BDC_OKCODE'
                fval     = '=ONLI' )  " Execute (F8)
              TO lt_bdcdata.

CALL TRANSACTION 'ZMYRPT' USING lt_bdcdata
  MODE 'N' UPDATE 'S'.

followupAnswerBank.question: How is SU01 used during selection screen development and testing?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SU01 SAP User Maintenance transaction hai. Selection screen development mein SU01 ka use testing ke liye hota hai: Restricted access test user create karo — jaise ek user jo sirf company code 1000 ka data dekh sakta hai. Yeh test user se report run karo — AUTHORITY-CHECK AT SELECTION-SCREEN properly fire ho raha hai ya nahi verify karo. Basis team SU01 use karta hai users banane ke liye — developer SU01 mein existing user ki roles check karta hai testing ke liye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SU01 is used during selection screen development to create or inspect test users for authorization testing. Developers use SU01 to create a test user with restricted roles — for example, a user authorized only for company code 1000 with display activity 03. This user is then used to test AUTHORITY-CHECK logic in AT SELECTION-SCREEN: attempting to run the report for company code 2000 should trigger the authorization error, while company code 1000 should succeed. SU01 also allows developers to check which authorization profiles are assigned to existing users.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: AUTHORITY-CHECK koda likhi AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein. Test karna tha ki restricted user ke liye error aata hai. SU01 mein ZTEST_RESTRICTED user dekha — role ZFIN_DISPLAY_1000 assigned tha (sirf company 1000). Isi user se report run kiya company 1000 ke saath — pass. Company 2000 ke saath — error message as expected. Authorization logic correct thi.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Developer account se authorization test karna — developer ke paas SAP_ALL ya broad access hoti hai. Authorization check developer account se hamesha pass karta hai. Restricted test user mandatory hai authorization testing ke liye.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SU01 Test User Setup (done via transaction):
" SU01 -> ZTEST_USER -> Roles tab -> Assign ZFIN_DISPLAY_1000
" Role ZFIN_DISPLAY_1000 authorization object:
"   F_BKPF_BUK: BUKRS=1000, ACTVT=03

" Test the authorization check with ZTEST_USER:
AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
    ID 'BUKRS' FIELD p_bukrs
    ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    MESSAGE |User { sy-uname } not authorized for company { p_bukrs }|
            TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.
" Expected: ZTEST_USER passes for 1000, fails for 2000

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the role of SE16/SE16N in selection screen development and debugging?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SE16 aur SE16N direct table browser hain. Selection screen development mein developer SE16 use karta hai: F4 help design karte waqt check karna ki source table mein actual data kya hai. Validation likhte waqt check karna ki T001 mein company code exist karta hai. PARAMETERS mein dictionary reference verify karna — table field ka exact naam. Debug karte waqt check karna ki database mein expected values hain ya nahi. SE16 testing aur debugging ka supporting tool hai — selection screen ka direct part nahi.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SE16 and SE16N are data browser transactions used to directly view table contents. During selection screen development, they are used to: verify master data exists for testing validation logic (e.g., confirm a company code exists in T001 before testing AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON validations), check the exact field names and types in a source table when designing custom F4 help (e.g., viewing T001W structure for plant F4), and debug production issues by confirming whether expected values exist in the database. While not directly related to selection screen code, SE16/SE16N are essential supporting tools for developers.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON p_werks validation likhni thi — SELECT from T001W. Pehle SE16N mein T001W khola — exact column naam confirm kiya 'WERKS'. Test data dekha — konse plant codes available hain testing ke liye. Phir SELECT likhya accurate column naam ke saath — zero guesswork.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: SE16 mein sensitive production data directly modify karne ki koshish karna — SE16 read-only browsing ke liye hai. Direct table modification ke liye ABAP code ya SM30 use karo, SE16 nahi.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SE16/SE16N usage during selection screen development:
" 1. Check T001 for valid company codes to use in testing:
"    SE16N -> Table T001 -> Execute -> Copy company codes for tests

" 2. Verify T001W structure for custom F4 design:
"    SE16N -> Table T001W -> Display structure -> note WERKS, NAME1 fields

" 3. Confirm data for validation testing:
"    SE16N -> Table KNA1 -> Filter KUNNR = 'CUST001' -> Confirm exists

" Code written with confirmed field names from SE16:
AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON p_werks.
  SELECT SINGLE werks FROM t001w    " Field name confirmed via SE16N
    INTO @DATA(lv_w)
    WHERE werks = @p_werks.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    MESSAGE |Plant { p_werks } does not exist| TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: How is SM30 related to selection screen configuration and master data management?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SM30 View Maintenance transaction hai — SAP customizing tables manage karne ke liye. Selection screen ke context mein SM30 direct nahi hai, lekin indirectly important hai: Agar selection screen ke F4 help source ek customizing table hai — SM30 se us table maintain hoti hai. Jaise plant list, company code list, material type list — yeh sab SM30 se maintained tables se aati hain. Agar F4 mein wrong data dikh raha hai toh SM30 mein source table check karo. Development mein SM30 use karke test data set up karo customizing tables mein.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SM30 is the View Maintenance transaction used to manage SAP customizing table entries. In the context of selection screens, SM30 is relevant when F4 help sources are customizing tables maintained by functional consultants or configuration teams. If a selection screen F4 field references a customizing table for its value list, incorrect F4 values are diagnosed by checking the underlying table in SM30. Developers also use SM30 to set up test data in customizing tables during development — for example, adding test entries to a Z-table that drives F4 help.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Custom F4 help for 'region' field tha — data custom Z-table ZREGION se aata tha. During testing F4 empty tha. SM30 mein ZREGION check kiya — dev system mein data nahi tha. SM30 se test entries add kiye — F4 ne data dikhaya. Root cause: data setup issue, code issue nahi.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: F4 empty hone pe immediately code debug karna — pehle SM30 ya SE16 mein source table check karo. Bahut baar F4 code correct hota hai lekin source table mein data nahi hota.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SM30 related to F4 source table maintenance:
" Custom F4 for region field reads from ZREGION table:
AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR p_region.
  DATA: lt_regions TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zregion,
        lt_ret     TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ddshretval.

  SELECT region_code region_name
    FROM zregion  " Maintained via SM30
    INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @lt_regions
    ORDER BY region_code.

  CALL FUNCTION 'F4IF_INT_TABLE_VALUE_REQUEST'
    EXPORTING retfield='REGION_CODE' dynprofield='P_REGION'
              dynpprog=sy-repid dynpnr=sy-dynnr
    TABLES value_tab=lt_regions return_tab=lt_ret.
" If F4 empty: check SM30 -> ZREGION for data
